raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The state passed AB 1248 in 2018, which allows students to wear traditional tribal regalia at graduation ceremonies.",
    "Students and families continue to face obstacles in exercising their right to wear tribal regalia during graduation despite the law.",
    "AB 945 was authored by James C. Ramos in 2021 to ensure compliance with the law regarding tribal regalia wear at graduations.",
    "AB 945 requires the California Department of Education to form a task force to work with schools on policies regarding the wearing of tribal regalia.",
    "Students' rights to wear tribal regalia during graduations have been disrespected by school administrators in California.",
    "Wearing tribal regalia at graduation ceremonies is significant for affirming Native identity and connection to ancestry and culture.",
    "High school graduations are significant celebrations in tribal communities and are a means to honor student achievements."
  ]
}
```
